name: The Bank Of Jackson
description_short: Centennial Bank (formerly The Bank of Jackson) is an FDIC-insured regional bank offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, and credit cards with rewards programs.
description_long: Centennial Bank operates as a family-owned regional bank with roots dating back to 1916. The Bank of Jackson customers transitioned to Centennial Bank on March 23, marking a significant organizational change. The bank maintains FDIC insurance backing, providing deposit protection up to applicable limits. Centennial Bank positions itself as an innovative community-focused financial institution serving both personal and business customers. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal banking products including multiple checking account tiers (Rewards Star, Gold Star Interest Checking, Rising Star Student Checking, 5 Star Protection Checking), savings accounts, money market accounts, CDs, and IRAs. Personal loan products include auto loans, debt consolidation loans, personal term loans, home mortgages, home equity loans, and construction loans. Credit card options are available for both personal and business use. Business customers can access business checking, business savings, business loans, merchant services, and cash management tools. Centennial Bank distinguishes itself through rewards-based account structures, particularly the Rewards Star Checking offering 5.00% APY on balances up to $10,000 (with lower rates for those not meeting qualifications). The bank offers Zelle payment capability, mobile banking with mobile deposit, ATM fee refunds, and an overdraft privilege program. They've partnered with Travelers Insurance for auto and home insurance offerings and maintain a pre-owned vehicle inventory for customer purchase. As a regional bank following a merger transition, Centennial Bank serves customers seeking traditional banking services with digital conveniences and rewards incentives. The 5.00% APY rates are competitive for checking accounts, though qualification requirements apply. The bank's primary limitations include regional availability (implied by community focus) and the recent transition from The Bank of Jackson, which may create ongoing operational adjustments.
services: Personal Checking (multiple tiers including Rewards Star, Gold Star Interest, Rising Star Student, 5 Star Protection)
Personal Savings (Rewards Savings, standard Savings, Money Market, Club Savings, CDs, IRAs)
Personal Loans (auto, debt consolidation, personal term, home mortgages, construction, home equity)
Personal Credit Cards with cash back and rewards options
Business Checking (Merchant Star Business, Merchant Gold Star variants)
Business Loans (term loans, operating lines of credit, commercial real estate, equipment, SBA, agriculture)
Mobile Banking with Mobile Deposit and Zelle payment capability
Cash Management and Merchant Card Services for business customers
Overdraft Privilege Program and ATM fee refunds
Travelers Auto and Home Insurance partnerships
Online Banking and Bill Pay with eStatements
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full faith and credit backing of U.S. Government
5.00% APY on Rewards Star Checking up to $10,000 balance — highly competitive rate
Multiple rewards account tiers (Rewards Star, Rewards Savings, Rewards Star Cash Back) catering to different customer needs
Comprehensive loan products including SBA loans, equipment financing, and agriculture loans for business clients
Mobile banking with mobile deposit and Zelle integration for convenient digital payments
ATM withdrawal fee refunds reducing out-of-network banking costs
Partnership with Travelers Insurance for competitive auto, home, and renters insurance rates
cons: Recent merger transition (March 23) from The Bank of Jackson to Centennial Bank may cause service disruptions or system integration issues
5.00% APY rate only applies to balances up to $10,000; balances exceeding this threshold earn only 0.01% APY — significant rate drop for larger deposits
Regional bank with implied geographic limitations on branch/ATM network availability
Website shows browser security vulnerability warning and mentions accessibility optimization in progress, suggesting potential technical infrastructure concerns
Limited information available about fee structures, minimum balance requirements, or account maintenance costs
best_for: Regional customers seeking high-yield checking accounts with rewards features and balances under $10,000
Small business owners needing comprehensive business banking, merchant services, and SBA loan access
Customers prioritizing community-focused, family-owned banking with FDIC insurance protection
Individuals interested in bundled insurance products alongside banking services
diagnosis: Centennial Bank is best for regional customers seeking FDIC-insured banking with competitive rewards checking rates and comprehensive loan/credit products. Primary caveat: the 5.00% APY rate is only available on balances up to $10,000, and the recent merger from The Bank of Jackson may affect service continuity during transition period.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Centennial Bank (formerly The Bank of Jackson) offers personal and business checking, savings, loans, and credit cards with rewards.
